professional.title: The Elevator
professional.prompt: You're in the elevator. Someone you half-recognize gets in.

choices.text: Smile and say hi. Keep it short.
Go for the full conversation. I can talk to anyone.
Phone comes out. Very important email happening right now.
Nod. Silence. We both understand.

professional.title: The Friday Ping
professional.prompt: Friday at 4:55. Someone pings you with a "quick question."

choices.text: Knock it out so it's not hanging over the weekend
"Can this wait until Monday?"
Pretend I didn't see it
Open it, sigh, start typing

professional.title: The Lunch Decision
professional.prompt: Nobody can decide where to go for lunch.

choices.text: I pick. Someone has to.
Throw out three options and make them choose
"I'm fine with whatever" and mean it
Already eating at my desk

student.title: The Invitation
student.prompt: Your friend wants to hang out but you told yourself you'd be productive tonight.

choices.text: I go. I wasn't going to be productive anyway.
Say no, then sit on my phone for three hours instead
Try to do both. Study at their place. Never works.
Actually stay in and get stuff done. It happens sometimes.

student.title: The Classroom
student.prompt: The professor asks a question to the whole class. You know the answer.

choices.text: Raise my hand. Why not.
Wait to see if someone else says it first
I know it but there's no way I'm volunteering
Start to raise my hand, then put it back down

student.title: The Library
student.prompt: You walk into the library and every seat is taken except the one next to someone you vaguely know.

choices.text: Sit down and say hey. It's just a seat.
Sit down, headphones in, pretend I didn't see them
Find a different spot even if it's worse
Leave and go somewhere else entirely

entrepreneur.title: The Dinner Question
entrepreneur.prompt: You're at dinner and someone asks "so what's new?"

choices.text: Tell them about the thing I'm working on. This is my opening.
Keep it vague. It's not ready to talk about yet.
Mention my day job. The project stays private for now.
"Not much, you?" and redirect

entrepreneur.title: The Competitor
entrepreneur.prompt: You see an ad for something weirdly similar to your idea.

choices.text: Click on it. I need to know everything about them.
Start making a mental list of how mine is different
Text someone: "Look at this. Should I be worried?"
Close the app. Ignorance is bliss right now.

entrepreneur.title: The Feedback
entrepreneur.prompt: Someone you respect gives you honest feedback on your idea and it's not all positive.

choices.text: Ask follow-up questions. I want specifics.
Thank them, go home, start adjusting
Thank them, go home, stare at the ceiling
Push back. I think they missed something.

parent.title: The Solo Drive
parent.prompt: You're in the car alone. No kids.

choices.text: Turn the music up. Loud.
Sit in silence for a minute. Just silence.
Call someone I've been meaning to catch up with
Drive-through. I've earned this.

parent.title: The Pickup Line
parent.prompt: Another parent at pickup is going on about their kid's coding camp, travel soccer, and Mandarin lessons.

choices.text: Nod along and ask questions. Maybe I should look into it.
Nod along while mentally planning dinner
"That's awesome" and change the subject
One-up them with something my kid did

parent.title: The Kid Quote
parent.prompt: Your kid says something so funny you wish you could tell someone right now.

choices.text: Text it to my partner or best friend immediately
Post it. The world needs to hear this.
Try to memorize it. I'll definitely forget.
Just laugh and stay in the moment

creative.title: The Afterglow
creative.prompt: You walk out of a museum, a concert, or a show that was genuinely incredible.

choices.text: Go home and start making something immediately
Call someone and talk about it for 20 minutes
Wander for a bit. Let it sit.
Pull out my phone and start taking notes

creative.title: The Compliment
creative.prompt: Someone gives you a real, genuine compliment on your work.

choices.text: "Thank you." Let it land.
"Oh, it's not that good" before I can stop myself
"Thanks! Here's what I'd change though..."
Screenshot it. I'm saving this for a bad day.

creative.title: The Late Night
creative.prompt: It's 1am. You're in the zone. You have stuff tomorrow morning.

choices.text: Keep going. The zone doesn't come on command.
Set a timer. 30 more minutes, then I'm done.
Stop, but leave everything exactly where it is for tomorrow
Keep going. Hate myself in the morning. Worth it.
